added Prettier (`prettier-plugin-tailwindcss`), ESLint (`eslint-plugin-better-tailwindcss`, React, import resolver), Knip (local schema), size/complexity rules; colocated configs under `app/`; renamed  `./.claude/CLAUDE.md`

```text
1	# Chroma Curve
2	
3	Monochromatic palette generator using a parabolic saturation curve for perceptual balance across lightness. Originally a CodePen (oxn-krtv/ExOwLBz).
4	
5	## Commands
6	
7	```bash
8	cd app
9	npm run dev      # Vite dev server
10	npm run build    # tsc -b && vite build
11	npm run lint     # ESLint
12	```
13	
14	## Architecture
15	
16	React 19 + TypeScript app built with Vite. All app code lives in `app/`.
17	
18	- `app/src/components/` - React components (controls, palette grid, export dialog, about)
19	- `app/src/lib/` - core logic: `palette.ts` (saturation curve), `colors.ts`, `export.ts`
20	- `app/src/store/paletteStore.ts` - Zustand state (hue, saturation, lightness range, layout)
21	- `app/src/hooks/` - `usePalette`, `useCopyToClipboard`, `useDynamicFavicon`
22	
23	## Key Dependencies
24	
25	- **chroma.js** - color manipulation and conversion
26	- **Tailwind CSS 4** - styling (via @tailwindcss/vite)
27	- **Zustand** - state management
28	- **Radix UI** - dialog, popover, slider, tabs primitives
29	- **Framer Motion** - animations
30	- **Phosphor Icons** - iconography
31	
32	## How It Works
33	
34	The core formula adjusts saturation across lightness values using a parabolic curve (see `getSaturation()`), creating neutrals that feel natural rather than flat. The Desmos visualization: https://www.desmos.com/calculator/02ufrfsuzy
35	
36	## Design Tokens
37	
38	### Type Scale
39	Custom scale defined in `app/src/index.css` via `@theme`. Use tokens, not hardcoded pixel sizes.
40	
41	`text-4xs` (8px) / `text-3xs` (9px) / `text-2xs` (10px) / `text-xs` (11px) / `text-sm` (12px) / `text-base` (14px) / `text-lg` (16px) / `text-xl` (18px) / `text-2xl` (21px) / `text-3xl` (24px) / `text-4xl` (30px)
42	
43	### Color Tokens
44	All colors use semantic tokens - never hardcode HSL values in components.
45	
46	- **Text**: `text-primary` (98%) / `text-secondary` (85%) / `text-muted` (72%) / `text-faint` (50%)
47	- **Surfaces**: `surface-base` (6%) / `surface-raised` (8%) / `surface-overlay` (10%) / `surface-hover` (12%) / `surface-active` (15%)
48	- **Borders**: `border-default` (12%) / `border-elevated` (15%)
49	- **Strokes (SVG)**: `stroke-grid` / `stroke-axis` / `stroke-secondary` / `stroke-primary`
50	
51	## Design Context
52	
53	### Users
54	Broad spectrum: UI/product designers building color systems, design engineers who care about perceptual accuracy, front-end developers needing neutral palettes for theming, and color theory enthusiasts drawn to the math. All share a baseline fluency with color - nobody needs hand-holding, but the tool should reward exploration at any depth.
55	
56	### Use Cases
57	1. **Monochromatic palettes** - mid-to-high saturation scales that feel cohesive and elegant, not cheap. The parabolic curve prevents the muddy midtones and blown-out extremes that make naive saturation scales look amateurish.
58	2. **Refined neutrals** - replacing flat grays with hue-tinted neutrals that carry subtle warmth or coolness. Fits into an existing brand system or elevates a neutral-heavy design. The curve ensures visual consistency across the lightness range so the tinting feels intentional, not random.
59	
60	### Brand Personality
61	**Precise, minimal, sharp.** The interface is a clinical instrument - it gets out of the way and lets the colors speak. Every pixel exists for a reason.
62	
63	### Emotional Goals
64	Confidence & control ("I trust the output"), quiet satisfaction ("every detail is considered"), creative flow ("tweaking until it feels right"), and intellectual curiosity ("I want to understand the curve").
65	
66	### Aesthetic Direction
67	- **Theme**: Dark-only. Near-black background. The generated colors are the only chromatic elements.
68	- **Typography**: Acumin Pro (body) + Unbounded (display) + Recursive Sans Linear Static (mono). Loaded via Adobe Fonts/Typekit (project `nbx6xju`). Tight tracking, small type sizes - information-dense without feeling cluttered.
69	- **Color values**: Use design tokens (`text-text-muted`, `fill-text-faint`, etc.) instead of hardcoded HSL or opacity tricks. Never use `white/60` or `rgba(255,255,255,0.6)` for text - opacity on text blends unpredictably with backgrounds.
70	- **Surfaces**: Subtle borders and fills. Backdrop blur on sticky elements. Depth through value contrast, not layered opacity.
71	- **Motion**: Purposeful and restrained. Framer Motion for meaningful transitions (dialogs, layout shifts). CSS for micro-interactions. `prefers-reduced-motion` respected globally.
72	- **Icons**: Phosphor Icons, used sparingly alongside text labels for clarity.
73	
74	### Design Principles
75	1. **Colors are the content** - the UI is a neutral stage. No decorative gradients, glows, or chrome that competes with the generated palette.
76	2. **Dense but legible** - pack information tight (small type, compact controls) but maintain clear hierarchy through opacity and weight, not size.
77	3. **Show the math** - expose the curve, the multipliers, the comparison strips. The technical underpinnings are a feature, not an implementation detail to hide.
78	4. **Instrument, not toy** - every control should feel precise and responsive. No playful bounces, no consumer-grade rounded-everything aesthetics.
79	5. **Earn trust through consistency** - uniform spacing scale, predictable control patterns, no surprises. If it looks the same, it works the same.
80	
81	### Anti-References
82	- Generic color pickers (Coolors, Adobe Color) - too playful, consumer-grade
83	- Dashboard/SaaS UI - card-heavy, rounded everything, admin-panel feel
84	- Academic/research tools - dry, text-heavy, unapproachable
85	- Overly decorative - gratuitous gradients/glows that distract from actual colors
86	
87	## Project Board
88	
89	This project uses a structured board system for project management.
90	
91	- **Ask before creating new board files** - don't auto-create tasks, ADRs, etc.
92	- Follow the board system spec for all documentation artifacts
93	- Use `/board:show` to see current board state
94	- Use `/board:add` to create new items
95	- Use `/board:update` after completing significant work to reconcile the board
96	- Use `/board:check` when starting work to find related board context
97
```
</details></tool-use>

1	import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from "react";
2	import chroma from "chroma-js";
3	import * as Popover from "@radix-ui/react-popover";
4	import { X } from "@phosphor-icons/react";
5	import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
6	import { parseTargetColor, formatTargetColor } from "../../lib/colors";
7	import type { ColorFormat } from "../../lib/colors";
8	
9	const PICKER_SIZE = 200;
10	
11	function ColorInput({
12	  currentHex,
13	  targetInput,
14	  targetFormat,
15	  hue,
16	  saturation,
17	  onApply,
18	}: {
19	  currentHex: string;
20	  targetInput: string | null;
21	  targetFormat: ColorFormat | null;
22	  hue: number;
23	  saturation: number;
24	  onApply: (
25	    hue: number,
26	    saturation: number,
27	    input: string,
28	    format: ColorFormat,
29	  ) => void;
30	}) {
31	  const [value, setValue] = useState("");
32	  const [isInvalid, setIsInvalid] = useState(false);
33	
34	  const targetFormatted = targetFormat
35	    ? formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, targetFormat)
36	    : currentHex;
37	
38	  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
39	    if (e.key === "Enter") {
40	      const result = parseTargetColor(value);
41	      if (result) {
42	        onApply(result.hue, result.saturation, value.trim(), result.format);
43	        setValue("");
44	        setIsInvalid(false);
45	      } else {
46	        setIsInvalid(true);
47	      }
48	    } else if (e.key === "Escape") {
49	      setValue("");
50	      setIsInvalid(false);
51	      (e.target as HTMLInputElement).blur();
52	    }
53	  };
54	
55	  return (
56	    <div className="mt-2">
57	      <div className="mb-1.5">
58	        {targetInput ? (
59	          <div className="flex flex-col gap-0.5">
60	            <div className="flex items-baseline gap-1.5 font-mono text-xs">
61	              <span className="text-text-secondary">{targetInput}</span>
62	              <span className="text-text-faint">→</span>
63	              <span className="text-text-primary">{targetFormatted}</span>
64	            </div>
65	            <span className="text-4xs text-text-faint tracking-wider uppercase">
66	              hue + saturation at L50
67	            </span>
68	          </div>
69	        ) : (
70	          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
71	            <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint tracking-wider uppercase">
72	              Target
73	            </span>
74	            <span className="text-text-secondary font-mono text-xs">
75	              {currentHex}
76	            </span>
77	          </div>
78	        )}
79	      </div>
80	      <input
81	        type="text"
82	        value={value}
83	        onChange={(e) => {
84	          setValue(e.target.value);
85	          setIsInvalid(false);
86	        }}
87	        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
88	        placeholder="#hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch()"
89	        className={`bg-surface-base text-text-primary placeholder:text-text-faint w-full border px-2 py-1.5 font-mono text-xs transition-colors outline-none ${isInvalid ? "border-red-500" : "border-border-default focus:border-text-faint"}`}
90	      />
91	    </div>
92	  );
93	}
94	
95	export function TargetSwatch() {
96	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
97	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
98	  const setHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setHue);
99	  const setSaturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setSaturation);
100	  const targetInput = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetInput);
101	  const targetFormat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetFormat);
102	  const targetHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetHue);
103	  const targetSat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetSat);
104	  const setTarget = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setTarget);
105	  const clearTarget = usePaletteStore((s) => s.clearTarget);
106	
107	  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);
108	  const draggingRef = useRef(false);
109	
110	  const targetActive = targetInput !== null;
111	  const targetDrifted =
112	    targetActive && (hue !== targetHue || saturation !== targetSat);
113	
114	  const color = `hsl(${hue}, ${saturation * 100}%, 50%)`;
115	  const currentHex = useMemo(
116	    () => chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5).hex(),
117	    [hue, saturation],
118	  );
119	  const ringColor = useMemo(
120	    () =>
121	      targetActive && !targetDrifted && targetHue !== null
122	        ? `hsl(${targetHue}, 80%, 55%)`
123	        : null,
124	    [targetActive, targetDrifted, targetHue],
125	  );
126	
127	  const drawPicker = useCallback(
128	    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement) => {
129	      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
130	      if (!ctx) return;
131	      const w = PICKER_SIZE;
132	      const h = PICKER_SIZE;
133	
134	      // Draw hue (x) vs saturation (y) at L=50%
135	      const imageData = ctx.createImageData(w, h);
136	      for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {
137	        for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
138	          const h360 = (x / (w - 1)) * 360;
139	          const s = 1 - y / (h - 1);
140	          // HSL to RGB conversion
141	          const c = (1 - Math.abs(2 * 0.5 - 1)) * s;
142	          const hp = h360 / 60;
143	          const x2 = c * (1 - Math.abs((hp % 2) - 1));
144	          let r1 = 0,
145	            g1 = 0,
146	            b1 = 0;
147	          if (hp < 1) {
148	            r1 = c;
149	            g1 = x2;
150	          } else if (hp < 2) {
151	            r1 = x2;
152	            g1 = c;
153	          } else if (hp < 3) {
154	            g1 = c;
155	            b1 = x2;
156	          } else if (hp < 4) {
157	            g1 = x2;
158	            b1 = c;
159	          } else if (hp < 5) {
160	            r1 = x2;
161	            b1 = c;
162	          } else {
163	            r1 = c;
164	            b1 = x2;
165	          }
166	          const m = 0.5 - c / 2;
167	          const i = (y * w + x) * 4;
168	          imageData.data[i] = Math.round((r1 + m) * 255);
169	          imageData.data[i + 1] = Math.round((g1 + m) * 255);
170	          imageData.data[i + 2] = Math.round((b1 + m) * 255);
171	          imageData.data[i + 3] = 255;
172	        }
173	      }
174	      ctx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);
175	
176	      // Draw crosshair at current hue/sat position
177	      const cx = (hue / 360) * (w - 1);
178	      const cy = (1 - saturation) * (h - 1);
179	      ctx.strokeStyle = "white";
180	      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
181	      ctx.beginPath();
182	      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 5, 0, Math.PI * 2);
183	      ctx.stroke();
184	      ctx.strokeStyle = "rgba(0,0,0,0.4)";
185	      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
186	      ctx.beginPath();
187	      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 6, 0, Math.PI * 2);
188	      ctx.stroke();
189	    },
190	    [hue, saturation],
191	  );
192	
193	  const handleCanvasRef = useCallback(
194	    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement | null) => {
195	      canvasRef.current = canvas;
196	      if (canvas) drawPicker(canvas);
197	    },
198	    [drawPicker],
199	  );
200	
201	  const pickFromCoords = useCallback(
202	    (clientX: number, clientY: number) => {
203	      const canvas = canvasRef.current;
204	      if (!canvas) return;
205	      const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
206	      if (
207	        clientX < rect.left ||
208	        clientX > rect.right ||
209	        clientY < rect.top ||
210	        clientY > rect.bottom
211	      )
212	        return;
213	      const x = (clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;
214	      const y = (clientY - rect.top) / rect.height;
215	      clearTarget();
216	      setHue(Math.round(x * 360));
217	      setSaturation(parseFloat((1 - y).toFixed(2)));
218	    },
219	    [setHue, setSaturation, clearTarget],
220	  );
221	
222	  const handleMouseDown = useCallback(
223	    (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLCanvasElement>) => {
224	      e.preventDefault();
225	      draggingRef.current = true;
226	      pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
227	    },
228	    [pickFromCoords],
229	  );
230	
231	  useEffect(() => {
232	    const handleMouseMove = (e: MouseEvent) => {
233	      if (draggingRef.current) pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
234	    };
235	    const handleMouseUp = () => {
236	      draggingRef.current = false;
237	    };
238	    document.addEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
239	    document.addEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
240	    return () => {
241	      document.removeEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
242	      document.removeEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
243	    };
244	  }, [pickFromCoords]);
245	
246	  return (
247	    <Popover.Root>
248	      <div className="group relative shrink-0 self-end">
249	        <Popover.Trigger asChild>
250	          <button
251	            type="button"
252	            className="flex cursor-pointer flex-col items-center gap-1"
253	            aria-label={
254	              targetActive
255	                ? `Target color: ${targetInput}${targetDrifted ? " (drifted)" : ""}`
256	                : `Current color: ${currentHex}`
257	            }
258	          >
259	            <div
260	              className="h-7 w-7 rounded-full transition-transform group-hover:scale-110"
261	              style={{
262	                backgroundColor: color,
263	                border: ringColor
264	                  ? `2px solid ${ringColor}`
265	                  : "2px solid var(--color-surface-base)",
266	                boxShadow: ringColor
267	                  ? `0 0 0 1px ${ringColor}`
268	                  : targetDrifted
269	                    ? "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.1), inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)"
270	                    : "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.15)",
271	                opacity: targetDrifted ? 0.6 : 1,
272	              }}
273	            />
274	            <span
275	              className={`text-3xs font-mono transition-colors ${
276	                targetActive && !targetDrifted
277	                  ? "text-text-secondary"
278	                  : "text-text-faint"
279	              }`}
280	            >
281	              {currentHex}
282	            </span>
283	          </button>
284	        </Popover.Trigger>
285	        {targetActive && (
286	          <button
287	            type="button"
288	            onClick={clearTarget}
289	            aria-label="Clear target color"
290	            className="bg-surface-overlay border-border-elevated text-text-secondary hover:text-text-primary hover:bg-surface-hover absolute -top-1 -right-1 z-10 flex h-4 w-4 items-center justify-center rounded-full border opacity-0 transition-opacity group-hover:opacity-100"
291	          >
292	            <X size={10} weight="bold" />
293	          </button>
294	        )}
295	      </div>
296	      <Popover.Portal>
297	        <Popover.Content
298	          sideOffset={8}
299	          className="bg-surface-overlay border-border-elevated z-[60] rounded-none border p-3 shadow-xl"
300	        >
301	          <canvas
302	            ref={handleCanvasRef}
303	            width={PICKER_SIZE}
304	            height={PICKER_SIZE}
305	            className="block cursor-crosshair"
306	            style={{ width: PICKER_SIZE, height: PICKER_SIZE }}
307	            onMouseDown={handleMouseDown}
308	          />
309	          <div className="text-text-faint mt-2 flex justify-between text-sm">
310	            <span>Hue {hue}°</span>
311	            <span>Sat {Math.round(saturation * 100)}%</span>
312	          </div>
313	          <ColorInput
314	            currentHex={currentHex}
315	            targetInput={targetInput}
316	            targetFormat={targetFormat}
317	            hue={hue}
318	            saturation={saturation}
319	            onApply={setTarget}
320	          />
321	          <Popover.Arrow className="fill-surface-overlay" />
322	        </Popover.Content>
323	      </Popover.Portal>
324	    </Popover.Root>
325	  );
326	}

1	import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
2	import { formatAllFormats, type AllFormats } from "../../lib/colors";
3	import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";
4	
5	const LABELS: Array<{ key: keyof AllFormats; label: string }> = [
6	  { key: "hex", label: "HEX" },
7	  { key: "rgb", label: "RGB" },
8	  { key: "hsl", label: "HSL" },
9	  { key: "oklch", label: "OKLCH" },
10	];
11	
12	export function TargetFormats() {
13	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
14	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
15	  const targetInput = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetInput);
16	  const targetHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetHue);
17	  const targetSat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetSat);
18	  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();
19	
20	  if (targetInput === null) return null;
21	
22	  const drifted = hue !== targetHue || saturation !== targetSat;
23	  const formats = formatAllFormats(hue, saturation);
24	
25	  return (
26	    <div
27	      className={`border-border-default mt-3 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-x-4 gap-y-1 border-t pt-3 ${drifted ? "opacity-55" : ""}`}
28	      aria-label="Target color formats"
29	    >
30	      <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint tracking-wider uppercase">
31	        Target{drifted ? " (drifted)" : ""}
32	      </span>
33	      {LABELS.map(({ key, label }) => {
34	        const value = formats[key];
35	        const isCopied = copiedLabel === label;
36	        return (
37	          <button
38	            key={key}
39	            type="button"
40	            onClick={() => copy(value, label)}
41	            className="hover:bg-surface-hover group/fmt flex cursor-pointer items-baseline gap-1.5 px-1.5 py-0.5 text-left transition-colors"
42	            aria-label={`Copy ${label} ${value}`}
43	          >
44	            <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint tracking-wider uppercase">
45	              {label}
46	            </span>
47	            <span className="text-text-secondary group-hover/fmt:text-text-primary font-mono text-xs tabular-nums">
48	              {isCopied ? "Copied" : value}
49	            </span>
50	          </button>
51	        );
52	      })}
53	    </div>
54	  );
55	}

1	import chroma from "chroma-js";
2	
3	const WCAG_AA_CONTRAST = 4.5;
4	
5	export function getContrastColor(bgColor: string): string {
6	  const whiteContrast = chroma.contrast("white", bgColor);
7	  return whiteContrast < WCAG_AA_CONTRAST ? "#1d1d1d" : "#ffffff";
8	}
9	
10	export type ColorFormat = "hex" | "hsl" | "rgb" | "oklch" | "named";
11	
12	/**
13	 * Parse a color string and extract hue (0-360), saturation (0-1), and input format.
14	 * Supports hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch(), and named CSS colors.
15	 */
16	export function parseTargetColor(
17	  input: string,
18	): { hue: number; saturation: number; format: ColorFormat } | null {
19	  const trimmed = input.trim();
20	  if (!trimmed) return null;
21	
22	  try {
23	    // oklch() - chroma.js can't parse this natively
24	    const oklchMatch = trimmed.match(
25	      /oklch\(\s*([\d.]+%?)\s+([\d.]+)\s+([\d.]+)\s*\)/i,
26	    );
27	    if (oklchMatch) {
28	      let l = parseFloat(oklchMatch[1]);
29	      if (oklchMatch[1].endsWith("%")) l /= 100;
30	      const c = parseFloat(oklchMatch[2]);
31	      const h = parseFloat(oklchMatch[3]);
32	      const [hue, sat] = chroma.oklch(l, c, h).hsl();
33	      return {
34	        hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
35	        saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
36	        format: "oklch",
37	      };
38	    }
39	
40	    // Detect format before parsing
41	    const lower = trimmed.toLowerCase();
42	    let format: ColorFormat = "named";
43	    if (lower.startsWith("#")) format = "hex";
44	    else if (lower.startsWith("hsl")) format = "hsl";
45	    else if (lower.startsWith("rgb")) format = "rgb";
46	
47	    const [hue, sat] = chroma(trimmed).hsl();
48	    return {
49	      hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
50	      saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
51	      format,
52	    };
53	  } catch {
54	    return null;
55	  }
56	}
57	
58	/**
59	 * Format a color at hue/sat/L=50 in the given format.
60	 */
61	export function formatTargetColor(
62	  hue: number,
63	  saturation: number,
64	  format: ColorFormat,
65	): string {
66	  const color = chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5);
67	  switch (format) {
68	    case "hsl":
69	      return `hsl(${hue},${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%,50%)`;
70	    case "rgb": {
71	      const [r, g, b] = color.rgb();
72	      return `rgb(${r},${g},${b})`;
73	    }
74	    case "oklch":
75	      return toOklchString(color.hex());
76	    default:
77	      return color.hex();
78	  }
79	}
80	
81	export function toOklchString(hex: string): string {
82	  const [l, c, h] = chroma(hex).oklch();
83	  const lR = Math.round(l * 1000) / 1000;
84	  const cR = Math.round((c || 0) * 1000) / 1000;
85	  const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
86	  return `oklch(${lR} ${cR} ${hR})`;
87	}
88	
89	export interface AllFormats {
90	  hex: string;
91	  rgb: string;
92	  hsl: string;
93	  oklch: string;
94	}
95	
96	/**
97	 * Return all four string representations of a color at hue/sat/L=50.
98	 */
99	export function formatAllFormats(hue: number, saturation: number): AllFormats {
100	  return {
101	    hex: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "hex"),
102	    rgb: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "rgb"),
103	    hsl: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "hsl"),
104	    oklch: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "oklch"),
105	  };
106	}
107

+import type { ExportFormat, ColorSpace } from "../../types/palette";
+
+interface FormatSelectorProps {
+  format: ExportFormat;
+  onFormatChange: (format: ExportFormat) => void;
+  space: ColorSpace;
+  onSpaceChange: (space: ColorSpace) => void;
+}
+
+const formats: { value: ExportFormat; label: string }[] = [
+  { value: "css", label: "CSS" },
+  { value: "tw4", label: "TW v4" },
+  { value: "tw3", label: "TW v3" },
+  { value: "scss", label: "SCSS" },
+  { value: "js", label: "JS/TS" },
+  { value: "json", label: "JSON" },
+  { value: "gpl", label: "GPL" },
+  { value: "text", label: "Text" },
+];
+
+const spaces: { value: ColorSpace; label: string }[] = [
+  { value: "hsl", label: "HSL" },
+  { value: "hex", label: "HEX" },
+  { value: "rgb", label: "RGB" },
+  { value: "hcl", label: "HCL" },
+  { value: "oklch", label: "OKLCH" },
+];
+
+const btnBase =
+  "flex-1 px-3 py-1.5 text-base font-medium rounded-none text-text-muted transition-colors hover:text-text-secondary cursor-pointer";
+const btnActive = "bg-surface-active text-white";
+
+export function FormatSelector({
+  format,
+  onFormatChange,
+  space,
+  onSpaceChange,
+}: FormatSelectorProps) {
+  return (
+    <div className="flex flex-col gap-3">
+      <div>
+        <span className="text-text-faint mb-1 block text-sm tracking-wider uppercase">
+          Format
+        </span>
+        <div
+          role="radiogroup"
+          aria-label="Export format"
+          className="bg-surface-overlay flex gap-1 rounded-none p-1"
+        >
+          {formats.map((f) => (
+            <button
+              key={f.value}
+              role="radio"
+              aria-checked={format === f.value}
+              className={`${btnBase} ${format === f.value ? btnActive : ""}`}
+              onClick={() => onFormatChange(f.value)}
+            >
+              {f.label}
+            </button>
+          ))}
+        </div>
+      </div>
+      <div>
+        <span className="text-text-faint mb-1 block text-sm tracking-wider uppercase">
+          Color Space
+        </span>
+        <div
+          role="radiogroup"
+          aria-label="Color space"
+          className="bg-surface-overlay flex gap-1 rounded-none p-1"
+        >
+          {spaces.map((s) => (
+            <button
+              key={s.value}
+              role="radio"
+              aria-checked={space === s.value}
+              className={`${btnBase} ${space === s.value ? btnActive : ""}`}
+              onClick={() => onSpaceChange(s.value)}
+            >
+              {s.label}
+            </button>
+          ))}
+        </div>
+      </div>
+    </div>
+  );
+}

```text
export const W = 400;
export const H = 400;
export const PAD = { top: 24, right: 24, bottom: 36, left: 48 };
export const PLOT_W = W - PAD.left - PAD.right;
export const PLOT_H = H - PAD.top - PAD.bottom;

export function toSvgX(lightness: number): number {
  return PAD.left + (lightness / 100) * PLOT_W;
}

export function toSvgY(value: number, yMin: number, yMax: number): number {
  return PAD.top + (1 - (value - yMin) / (yMax - yMin)) * PLOT_H;
}
     453 app/src/components/LightnessChart/chartConstants.ts

 `./scripts/contrast-audit.mjs`

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env node
2	
3	/**
4	 * Parses text and surface color tokens from index.css and prints
5	 * a contrast-ratio matrix, flagging AA (≥4.5:1) and AAA (≥7:1).
6	 *
7	 * Usage: node scripts/contrast-audit.mjs
8	 */
9	
10	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
11	import { resolve, dirname } from "node:path";
12	import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
13	
14	const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
15	const css = readFileSync(
16	  resolve(__dirname, "../app/src/index.css"),
17	  "utf-8",
18	);
19	
20	// Extract HSL lightness from achromatic tokens: hsl(0 0% L%)
21	const tokenRe = /--color-(text|surface)-([\w-]+):\s*hsl\(\s*0\s+0%\s+([\d.]+)%\s*\)/g;
22	
23	const text = [];
24	const surfaces = [];
25	
26	for (const m of css.matchAll(tokenRe)) {
27	  const [, group, name, lightness] = m;
28	  const entry = { name, pct: parseFloat(lightness) };
29	  if (group === "text") text.push(entry);
30	  else surfaces.push(entry);
31	}
32	
33	if (!text.length || !surfaces.length) {
34	  console.error("No tokens found — check index.css format");
35	  process.exit(1);
36	}
37	
38	// sRGB linearization
39	const linearize = (c) =>
40	  c <= 0.04045 ? c / 12.92 : Math.pow((c + 0.055) / 1.055, 2.4);
41	const luminance = (pct) => linearize(pct / 100);
42	const contrast = (l1, l2) => {
43	  const [a, b] = l1 > l2 ? [l1, l2] : [l2, l1];
44	  return (a + 0.05) / (b + 0.05);
45	};
46	
47	// ANSI colors
48	const green = (s) => `\x1b[32m${s}\x1b[0m`;
49	const yellow = (s) => `\x1b[33m${s}\x1b[0m`;
50	const red = (s) => `\x1b[31m${s}\x1b[0m`;
51	
52	// Layout
53	const COL = 14;
54	const LABEL = 26;
55	const pad = (s) => s.padStart(COL);
56	const label = (s) => s.padEnd(LABEL);
57	
58	// Header
59	console.log(label("") + text.map((t) => pad(t.name)).join(""));
60	console.log(label("") + text.map((t) => pad(`L:${t.pct}%`)).join(""));
61	console.log("-".repeat(LABEL + text.length * COL));
62	
63	// Rows
64	let failures = 0;
65	
66	for (const bg of surfaces) {
67	  const bLum = luminance(bg.pct);
68	  let row = label(`${bg.name} (L:${bg.pct}%)`);
69	  for (const fg of text) {
70	    const r = contrast(luminance(fg.pct), bLum);
71	    let tag = "";
72	    const val = `${r.toFixed(1)}:1`;
73	    let level, colorize;
74	    if (r >= 7) {
75	      level = "AAA ";
76	      colorize = green;
77	    } else if (r >= 4.5) {
78	      level = "AA  ";
79	      colorize = yellow;
80	    } else {
81	      level = "FAIL";
82	      colorize = red;
83	      failures++;
84	    }
85	    row += colorize(pad(`${val} ${level}`));
86	  }
87	  console.log(row);
88	}
89	
90	console.log("-".repeat(LABEL + text.length * COL));
91	console.log(
92	  `\n${failures === 0 ? "All combinations meet AA." : `${failures} combination(s) below AA (4.5:1).`}`,
93	);
94	
95	process.exit(failures > 0 ? 1 : 0);
96
```
</details></tool-use>

  --text-3xl: 1.5rem; /* 24px */
  --text-4xl: 1.875rem; /* 30px */

  /* Text */
  --color-text-primary: hsl(0 0% 98%);
  --color-text-secondary: hsl(0 0% 85%);
  --color-text-muted: hsl(0 0% 72%);
  --color-text-faint: hsl(0 0% 48%);

  /* Surfaces */
  --color-surface-base: hsl(0 0% 4%);
  --color-surface-raised: hsl(0 0% 8%);
  --color-surface-overlay: hsl(0 0% 10%);
  --color-surface-hover: hsl(0 0% 12%);
  --color-surface-active: hsl(0 0% 15%);
  --color-surface-active-hover: hsl(0 0% 20%);

  /* Borders */
  --color-border-default: hsl(0 0% 12%);
  --color-border-elevated: hsl(0 0% 15%);

  /* Strokes (SVG) */
  --color-stroke-grid: hsl(0 0% 12%);
  --color-stroke-axis: hsl(0 0% 17%);
  --color-stroke-secondary: hsl(0 0% 44%);
  --color-stroke-primary: hsl(0 0% 72%);
}

@layer base {
  *,
  *::before,
  *::after {
    box-sizing: border-box;
  }

  html {
    font-size: 16px;
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Get CLAUDE.md Color Tokens section for comparison

```bash
# Check CLAUDE.md Color Tokens section vs index.css
grep -A 20 'Color Tokens' /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/.claude/CLAUDE.md
```

```text
### Color Tokens
All colors use semantic tokens - never hardcode HSL values in components.

- **Text**: `text-primary` (98%) / `text-secondary` (85%) / `text-muted` (72%) / `text-faint` (50%)
- **Surfaces**: `surface-base` (6%) / `surface-raised` (8%) / `surface-overlay` (10%) / `surface-hover` (12%) / `surface-active` (15%)
- **Borders**: `border-default` (12%) / `border-elevated` (15%)
- **Strokes (SVG)**: `stroke-grid` / `stroke-axis` / `stroke-secondary` / `stroke-primary`

## Design Context

### Users
Broad spectrum: UI/product designers building color systems, design engineers who care about perceptual accuracy, front-end developers needing neutral palettes for theming, and color theory enthusiasts drawn to the math. All share a baseline fluency with color - nobody needs hand-holding, but the tool should reward exploration at any depth.

### Use Cases
1. **Monochromatic palettes** - mid-to-high saturation scales that feel cohesive and elegant, not cheap. The parabolic curve prevents the muddy midtones and blown-out extremes that make naive saturation scales look amateurish.
2. **Refined neutrals** - replacing flat grays with hue-tinted neutrals that carry subtle warmth or coolness. Fits into an existing brand system or elevates a neutral-heavy design. The curve ensures visual consistency across the lightness range so the tinting feels intentional, not random.

### Brand Personality
**Precise, minimal, sharp.** The interface is a clinical instrument - it gets out of the way and lets the colors speak. Every pixel exists for a reason.

### Emotional Goals
```
</details></tool-use>

+import { W, H, PAD, PLOT_W, PLOT_H, toSvgX, toSvgY } from "./chartConstants";
+
+interface GridLine {
+  value: number;
+  label: string;
+}
+
+interface Dot {
+  key: Key;
+  lightness: number;
+  value: number;
+  fill: string;
+}
+
+interface Point {
+  lightness: number;
+  value: number;
+}
+
+interface LightnessChartProps {
+  titleId: string;
+  title: string;
+  yAxisLabel: string;
+  yDomain: [yMin: number, yMax: number];
+  gridLines: GridLine[];
+  baseline: { value: number };
+  curvePoints: Point[];
+  dots: Dot[];
+  showDots: boolean;
+}
+
+const X_GRID_VALUES = [0, 25, 50, 75, 100];
+
+export function LightnessChart({
+  titleId,
+  title,
+  yAxisLabel,
+  yDomain,
+  gridLines,
+  baseline,
+  curvePoints,
+  dots,
+  showDots,
+}: LightnessChartProps) {
+  const [yMin, yMax] = yDomain;
+
+  const projectedGridLines = gridLines.map((g) => ({
+    ...g,
+    y: toSvgY(g.value, yMin, yMax),
+  }));
+
+  const verticalGridTop =
+    projectedGridLines.length > 0
+      ? Math.min(...projectedGridLines.map((g) => g.y))
+      : PAD.top;
+
+  const curvePath = curvePoints
+    .map((p, i) => {
+      const cmd = i === 0 ? "M" : "L";
+      return `${cmd}${toSvgX(p.lightness).toFixed(2)},${toSvgY(p.value, yMin, yMax).toFixed(2)}`;
+    })
+    .join("");
+
+  const baselineY = toSvgY(baseline.value, yMin, yMax);
+
+  return (
+    <div>
+      <svg
+        viewBox={`0 0 ${W} ${H}`}
+        className="h-auto w-full"
+        role="img"
+        aria-labelledby={titleId}
+      >
```
</details></tool-use>

+import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
+import * as Tabs from "@radix-ui/react-tabs";
+import chroma from "chroma-js";
+import { Triangle } from "@phosphor-icons/react";
+import { SaturationCurve } from "../SaturationCurve";
+import { EffectiveSaturation } from "../EffectiveSaturation";
+import { ToggleSwitch } from "../ToggleSwitch";
+import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
+import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";
+
+interface ChartTabsProps {
+  entries: ColorEntry[];
+}
+
+export function ChartTabs({ entries }: ChartTabsProps) {
+  const [showDots, setShowDots] = useState(true);
+  const [showCompare, setShowCompare] = useState(false);
+  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
+  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
+
+  const { unadjustedColors, adjustedColors } = useMemo(
+    () => ({
+      unadjustedColors: entries.map((e) =>
+        chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, e.lightness / 100).hex(),
+      ),
+      adjustedColors: entries.map((e) => e.hex),
+    }),
+    [entries, hue, saturation],
+  );
+
+  return (
+    <div className="border-border-default bg-surface-raised mx-4 overflow-hidden rounded-none border md:mx-0 lg:mr-4">
+      <div className="flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-2 md:hidden lg:flex">
+        <h2 className="text-text-muted w-full text-center text-sm font-semibold tracking-wider uppercase">
+          Saturation Curve
+        </h2>
+      </div>
+      <Tabs.Root defaultValue="adjustment">
+        <Tabs.List className="border-border-default flex border-y">
+          <Tabs.Trigger
+            value="adjustment"
+            className="text-text-faint data-[state=active]:text-text-primary border-border-default flex-1 cursor-pointer border-r px-4 py-2 text-xs font-medium tracking-wider uppercase transition-colors md:px-2 md:py-2 md:tracking-normal lg:px-4 lg:py-2 lg:tracking-wider"
+          >
+            Adjustment
+          </Tabs.Trigger>
+          <Tabs.Trigger
+            value="computed"
+            className="text-text-faint data-[state=active]:text-text-primary flex-1 cursor-pointer px-4 py-2 text-xs font-medium tracking-wider uppercase transition-colors md:tracking-normal lg:tracking-wider"
+          >
+            Computed
+          </Tabs.Trigger>
+        </Tabs.List>
+        <Tabs.Content
+          value="adjustment"
+          forceMount
+          className="data-[state=inactive]:hidden"
+        >
+          <h3 className="sr-only">
+            Adjustment <span>multiplier</span>
+          </h3>
+          <SaturationCurve entries={entries} showDots={showDots} />
+        </Tabs.Content>
+        <Tabs.Content
+          value="computed"
+          forceMount
+          className="data-[state=inactive]:hidden"
+        >
+          <h3 className="sr-only">
+            Computed <span>values</span>
+          </h3>
+          <EffectiveSaturation entries={entries} showDots={showDots} />
+        </Tabs.Content>
+      </Tabs.Root>
+      <div className="flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-2 md:hidden lg:flex">
+        <button
+          type="button"
+          onClick={() => setShowCompare(!showCompare)}
+          className="text-text-faint hover:text-text-muted cursor-pointer text-sm font-semibold transition-colors select-none md:hidden lg:inline-flex lg:items-center lg:gap-1"
+        >
+          <Triangle
+            size={7}
+            weight="fill"
+            className={`transition-transform ${showCompare ? "rotate-180" : "rotate-90"}`}
+          />{" "}
+          Compare
+        </button>
+        <ToggleSwitch label="steps" checked={showDots} onChange={setShowDots} />
+      </div>
+      {showCompare && (
+        <div className="space-y-2 px-4 pb-3 md:hidden lg:block">
+          <div>
+            <span className="text-text-faint mb-1 block text-xs">
+              Unadjusted
+            </span>
+            <div className="grid h-6 auto-cols-fr grid-flow-col overflow-hidden rounded-none">
+              {unadjustedColors.map((c, i) => (
+                <div key={i} style={{ backgroundColor: c }} />
+              ))}
+            </div>
+          </div>
+          <div>
+            <span className="text-text-faint mb-1 block text-xs">Adjusted</span>
+            <div className="grid h-6 auto-cols-fr grid-flow-col overflow-hidden rounded-none">
+              {adjustedColors.map((c, i) => (
+                <div key={i} style={{ backgroundColor: c }} />
+              ))}
+            </div>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+      )}
+    </div>
+  );
+}

Review complete. Here's the summary:

**Token review — 1 blocker, 3 medium, 2 low, 2 doc-drift nits.**

**Blocker (H1):** `text-faint` was changed from 50% → 48% lightness. The contrast audit confirms this breaks WCAG AA on 5 of 6 surface pairings (`raised`, `overlay`, `hover`, `active`, `active-hover`). One-line fix in `app/src/index.css`: bump back to 50% or 52%.

**Medium:**
- `TargetSwatch.tsx:184` — canvas `strokeStyle = "rgba(0,0,0,0.4)"` (contextually appropriate, accept with comment)
- `TargetSwatch.tsx:269-270` — inline `boxShadow` using `rgba()` for drifted-state ring
- `TargetFormats.tsx:27` — `opacity-55` on a text-containing div degrades faint text below AA when drifted

**Low (pre-existing, not introduced by this PR):** `ring-white/50` focus rings and `bg-white` toggle knob in SliderBase/LightnessRange/SaturationSlider/ToggleSwitch.

**Doc-drift:** CLAUDE.md has stale values — `text-faint (50%)` should be 48%, `surface-base (6%)` should be 4%, and `surface-active-hover` is missing entirely.

<teammate-message teammate_id="tokens-reviewer" color="yellow" summary="Design tokens review complete — findings report">
Design tokens review complete. Here are the full findings:

---

## Summary

Token consolidation (6→4 text tokens) is clean. AboutButton is well-disciplined with semantic tokens throughout. No orphaned references to removed tokens (text-tertiary, text-subtle). The main issues are: `white/NN` opacity on focus rings and UI chrome (pre-existing, not introduced by this PR), `rgba()` in TargetSwatch inline styles (medium), `text-faint` failing AA contrast on raised surfaces (contrast audit result), and two doc-drift issues in CLAUDE.md.

---

## Findings

### Critical
None.

---

### High

**[H1] `text-faint` fails WCAG AA on raised/overlay/hover/active surfaces — contrast-audit.mjs confirms 5 failing pairs**

- `text-faint` is now `hsl(0 0% 48%)` (was 50%). This change dropped it below AA on every surface above `surface-base`.
- Failing pairs from `node scripts/contrast-audit.mjs`:
  - `faint` on `raised (8%)`: 4.3:1 FAIL
  - `faint` on `overlay (10%)`: 4.1:1 FAIL
  - `faint` on `hover (12%)`: 3.9:1 FAIL
  - `faint` on `active (15%)`: 3.5:1 FAIL
  - `faint` on `active-hover (20%)`: 3.0:1 FAIL
- `text-faint` appears on many elements rendered over `surface-overlay` and `surface-raised` (AboutButton labels, header descriptors, TargetFormats, etc.)
- Fix: bump `text-faint` back to `hsl(0 0% 50%)` (restores AA on `raised`, marginal on `overlay`), or to `hsl(0 0% 52%)` to clear AA on `overlay` as well. Alternatively, audit every `text-faint` usage and replace with `text-muted` where it sits on a raised surface.

---

### Medium

**[M1] `TargetSwatch.tsx:184` — `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` on canvas `strokeStyle` (not text)**
- File: `app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:184`
- `ctx.strokeStyle = "rgba(0,0,0,0.4)"` — this is a canvas 2D stroke, not a text color, but it's a raw `rgba()` value rather than a token.
- Low harm (it's a canvas rendering context, not CSS), but worth converting to an explicit gray. Closest token: `stroke-grid` (`hsl(0 0% 12%)`). However the alpha compositing here over a variable background makes the raw rgba contextually appropriate.
- Recommendation: Accept as-is with a comment; it's an intentional alpha-composited canvas stroke, not a token-eligible context.

**[M2] `TargetSwatch.tsx:269-270` — inline `box-shadow` using `rgba(255,255,255,0.1)` and `rgba(0,0,0,0.3)` for a border-like ring**
- File: `app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:269-270`
- These are inline style `boxShadow` values used as visual ring indicators for the drifted-target state. Not text, but does use raw opacity-over-color patterns.
- Fix: extract to a CSS custom property or use `box-shadow` with explicit gray values from the token scale (e.g. `hsl(0 0% 100% / 0.1)` → `hsl(0 0% 15%)` on the token scale, though the alpha compositing effect would differ). This is a context where exact translucency is intentional, so a comment is acceptable if not tokenized.

**[M3] `TargetFormats.tsx:27` — `opacity-55` on a containing div to dim "drifted" state**
- File: `app/src/components/TargetFormats/TargetFormats.tsx:27`
- Using `opacity-55` on a block element (not text directly), but this degrades text contrast within that element when drifted. `text-faint` inside a 55% opacity wrapper on a raised surface would be well below AA.
- Fix: instead of dimming the whole container with opacity, use `text-text-faint` at the token level and change the explicit text color tokens to a dimmer value for the drifted state, or use a reduced-opacity token from the surface scale on background rather than the opacity utility on text-containing elements.

---

### Low

**[L1] Pre-existing: `ring-white/50` and `ring-white/20` for focus rings across multiple components**
- Files: `SliderBase.tsx:54`, `LightnessRange.tsx:87,91`, `SaturationSlider.tsx:54`, `ColorSwatch.tsx:39`, `AboutButton.tsx:655,668,705,810`
- These are focus-ring and hover-ring styles (not text colors). The policy states "NEVER use white/NN for text" — focus rings and UI chrome are edge cases, but the project standard should be consistent.
- These appear pre-existing (not introduced in this PR), but the new AboutButton additions at lines 655, 668, 705, 810 bring more instances in.
- Recommendation: Medium priority to tokenize focus rings (e.g. `--color-focus-ring: hsl(0 0% 100% / 0.5)`), but since these are not text colors and the rule's intent is about text legibility, these are lower severity. Flag for a follow-up token audit.

**[L2] `ToggleSwitch.tsx:42` — `bg-white` and `rgba()` for toggle knob**
- File: `app/src/components/ToggleSwitch/ToggleSwitch.tsx:42`
- `bg-white` and `shadow-[0_0_0_1px_rgba(0,0,0,0.3),0_2px_4px_rgba(0,0,0,0.4)]` are used on the toggle knob (not text). Pre-existing, not introduced in this PR.

---

### Nit / Doc Drift

**[N1] CLAUDE.md `text-faint` listed as 50% but token is now 48%**
- `CLAUDE.md` Color Tokens: `text-faint (50%)`
- `index.css`: `--color-text-primary: hsl(0 0% 48%)`
- Fix: update CLAUDE.md to `text-faint (48%)` or adjust the token back to 50%.

**[N2] CLAUDE.md missing `surface-active-hover` token and `surface-base` lightness is stale**
- `CLAUDE.md` Surfaces: `surface-base (6%)` — actual token is now `hsl(0 0% 4%)`
- `CLAUDE.md` Surfaces: does not list `surface-active-hover` (20%) — this token exists in `index.css` and is used in components
- Fix: update CLAUDE.md to `surface-base (4%)` and add `surface-active-hover (20%)` to the surfaces list.

---

## Contrast Audit Output

```
                             primary     secondary         muted         faint
                               L:98%         L:85%         L:72%         L:48%
------------------------------------------------------------------------
base (L:4%)           18.9:1 AAA   14.0:1 AAA    9.9:1 AAA    4.6:1 AA
raised (L:8%)         17.6:1 AAA   13.0:1 AAA    9.2:1 AAA    4.3:1 FAIL
overlay (L:10%)       16.7:1 AAA   12.4:1 AAA    8.8:1 AAA    4.1:1 FAIL
hover (L:12%)         15.9:1 AAA   11.7:1 AAA    8.3:1 AAA    3.9:1 FAIL
active (L:15%)        14.4:1 AAA   10.7:1 AAA    7.6:1 AAA    3.5:1 FAIL
active-hover (L:20%)  12.1:1 AAA    8.9:1 AAA    6.3:1 AA     3.0:1 FAIL

5 combination(s) below AA (4.5:1).
```

Script exits with code 1. primary/secondary/muted all pass AAA or AA. Only faint fails.

---

## Blocking?

H1 (text-faint contrast failures) is the only finding that should block merge. The token value change from 50%→48% is what introduced the failures — a one-line fix in index.css (and CLAUDE.md) resolves it. Everything else is either pre-existing, context-appropriate, or doc-only.
</teammate-message>

cd /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app && node --input-type=module << 'EOF'
import chroma from './node_modules/chroma-js/index.js';

// Test: what does hsl() return on chroma.js when hue is 360?
// pickFromCoords can produce hue=360 when x=1.0
const c = chroma.hsl(360, 0.8, 0.5);
console.log('hsl(360,...) hex:', c.hex());
console.log('hsl(360,...).hsl():', c.hsl());

// Test: oklch c=NaN case (when chroma is very close to 0)
// white has c=3.88e-5, not NaN -- so c||0 works BUT
// verify formatTargetColor with white (sat=0)
const gray = chroma.hsl(0, 0, 0.5);
const [r,g,b] = gray.rgb();
console.log('gray rgb:', r, g, b);

// Verify null vs NaN distinction in parseTargetColor
// chroma('gray').hsl() returns [null, 0] 
// isNaN(null) = false! So hue would be set to null, not 0
const hue = null;
console.log('isNaN(null):', isNaN(hue as unknown as number)); // false
console.log('Math.round(null):', Math.round(hue as unknown as number)); // 0 - ok, Math.round coerces
EOF

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Verify null hue/sat handling with Math.round chain

```bash
cd /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app && node --input-type=module << 'EOF'
import chroma from './node_modules/chroma-js/index.js';

// isNaN(null) check
const hue = null;
console.log('isNaN(null):', isNaN(hue)); // false - BUG: null is not NaN
console.log('Math.round(null):', Math.round(hue)); // 0 - this is OK

// So in parseTargetColor: isNaN(hue) where hue=null -> false
// Result: hue stays null, Math.round(null) = 0. 
// Wait - it's Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue)
// = Math.round(false ? 0 : null) = Math.round(null) = 0
// So it resolves to 0 correctly! Not a bug after all.

// But what about saturation=null?
const sat = null;
console.log('sat null chain:', Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100);
// = Math.round((false ? 0 : null) * 100) / 100 = Math.round(null * 100)/100 = 0/100 = 0. OK!

// Test hue=360 from pickFromCoords: does the palette handle it?
// hue 360 = hue 0, should be fine. But store stores 360.
const c360 = chroma.hsl(360, 0.5, 0.5);
console.log('hsl(360) hex:', c360.hex());
EOF
```

EOF

## Component Refactor Review — PR #5 (feat/target-color-input)

**Summary:** The folder-per-component restructure is clean. Build passes (`tsc -b && vite build`) with no errors. No stale imports, no circular deps, no dead exports. One nit about `chartConstants.ts`.

---

### Findings

**1. Stale imports — PASS**
`rg 'components/controls/'` and `rg 'components/curves/'` across `app/src` returned zero hits. All call sites have been updated to the new paths.

**2. Barrel consistency — PASS**
All 24 `index.ts` barrels follow an identical pattern: a single named re-export (`export { ComponentName } from "./ComponentName"`). No internal helpers leak through any barrel. Fully consistent.

**3. Default vs named exports — PASS**
Every component file uses `export function ComponentName(...)` (named function export). No `export default` anywhere in components. Every barrel re-exports the named export. 100% consistent.

**4. Props/contract breaks — PASS**
Spot-checked all new/extracted components against their call sites:
- `ChartTabs({ entries })` — called correctly in `ControlBar.tsx` and `App.tsx`
- `SaturationCurve({ entries, showDots })` — called correctly inside `ChartTabs`
- `EffectiveSaturation({ entries, showDots })` — called correctly inside `ChartTabs`
- `ToggleSwitch({ label, checked, onChange })` — called correctly in `ChartTabs` and `PaletteGrid`
- `LightnessChart({ titleId, title, yAxisLabel, yDomain, gridLines, baseline, curvePoints, dots, showDots })` — fully internal to `SaturationCurve`/`EffectiveSaturation`, no external call sites to break
- `ControlBar()` — no props, called in `App.tsx` correctly
- `InlineSelect<T>` — generic preserved, called in `PaletteGrid` correctly
No contract breaks found.

**5. chartConstants.ts — Nit (Low)**
File: `app/src/components/LightnessChart/chartConstants.ts`
Status: **Not empty** — 453 bytes, contains `W`, `H`, `PAD`, `PLOT_W`, `PLOT_H`, `toSvgX`, `toSvgY`. The report of "0 bytes new file" in git stat is misleading; it's a rename+move from `components/curves/chartConstants.ts` with zero content change (shown as `0` additions/deletions in the rename stat). The file is actively imported by both `LightnessChart.tsx` and indirectly through it. No action needed — this is not a dead artifact.

**6. Circular deps — PASS**
`npx madge --circular src` reports: `✔ No circular dependency found!`

**7. Dead exports — PASS**
`npm run knip` exited cleanly with no output (no dead exports reported).

---

**Verdict:** Refactor is structurally sound. All 7 checks pass. No bugs, no broken contracts, no orphaned files. Ready to merge.

User has strong saved feedback on SSOT violations.

**[HIGH] Codacy ESLint version mismatch**
- `.codacy/codacy.yaml:9` pins `eslint@8.57.0`
- Installed/used version is `9.39.4` (flat config format)

**[MEDIUM] `config.sh` hard-requires `.env.development.local` — no guard**

**[MEDIUM] Root `package-lock.json` has stale `prettier-eslint` entry**
- `package.json` (root) only declares `wrangler` as a devDependency
- The lockfile includes `prettier-eslint@^16.4.2` — a ghost entry that doesn't match the manifest
- This will generate an npm audit warning and cause `npm ci` to fail in CI (lockfile mismatch). Fix: run `npm install` at root to regenerate the lockfile.

**[LOW] `better-tailwindcss` warnings not treated as errors — 26 fixable warnings open**
- `npm run verify` exits 0 but prints 26 `better-tailwindcss` warnings (canonical class simplifications, one duplicate class)
- All are auto-fixable via `npm run lint:fix`. Not a blocker, but worth a cleanup pass.

**[LOW] `contrast-audit.mjs` is not wired into any npm script**
- `scripts/contrast-audit.mjs` exists but no `contrast`, `audit`, or verify-step references it in `app/package.json` or root `package.json`
- If it's meant to be part of CI or verify pipeline, it needs a script entry. If it's a one-off dev tool, that's fine — just worth confirming intent.

**[NIT] `eslint.config.js` override globs include Astro/Next patterns this repo doesn't use**
- `app/eslint.config.js:92-98`: override includes `src/pages/**/*.astro`, `astro.config.*`, `next.config.*` — this is a React/Vite app, not Astro or Next
- No functional harm, but these are dead globs that suggest copy-paste from a template. Worth trimming for clarity.

**[NIT] No `.nvmrc` or `engines` field**
- Global CLAUDE.md prefers Node ^24 LTS; `@types/node` is pinned to `^24.12.0` in app devDeps (consistent), but there's no `.nvmrc` or `engines` field to enforce this for other contributors

---

- `npm run verify` — exits 0 (lint: 26 warns/0 errors, format: clean, knip: clean, typecheck: clean)
- `npm run build` — exits 0 (vite 7, chunk size warning is cosmetic)

Project rules prohibit `rgba(` in component styles; design tokens must be used. The `border-stroke-grid` / `border-border-default` tokens exist for this.\n\nFix: move these to Tailwind `ring-*` / `shadow-*` utilities using CSS variable tokens, or at minimum use `var(--color-border-default)` style references.\n\n---\n\n**M3 — No ARIA live region for copy feedback in TargetFormats**\n`app/src/components/TargetFormats/TargetFormats.tsx:33-51`\n\nWhen a format button is clicked, the label text changes from e.g. \"rgb(180,90,127)\" to \"Copied\". This is purely visual — screen readers get no announcement. Users relying on assistive technology have no confirmation their action succeeded.\n\nFix: add `aria-live=\"polite\"` and `aria-atomic=\"true\"` to the value `<span>` (line 47), or wrap the entire format row container with a live region.\n\n---\n\n### LOW\n\n**L1 — `oklch()` parser does not handle modern slash-alpha syntax**\n`app/src/lib/colors.ts:24-27`\n\nThe regex `/oklch\\(\\s*([\\d.]+%?)\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s+([\\d.]+)\\s*\\)/i` rejects `oklch(0.7 0.15 200 / 0.5)` (CSS Color Level 4 alpha syntax) — it returns `null` and the input is flagged invalid. A user pasting from a modern design tool that emits oklch with alpha would see a false parse failure.\n\nFix: extend the regex to optionally match `\\s*(?:\\/\\s*[\\d.]+%?)?\\s*` before the closing paren, then ignore the alpha channel for hue/sat extraction (since the tool operates at fixed L=50).\n\n---\n\n**L2 — `formatTargetColor` HSL output omits spaces after separators**\n`app/src/lib/colors.ts:69`\n\n```ts\nreturn `hsl(${hue},${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%,50%)`;\n```\n\nProduces `hsl(180,50%,50%)` — no spaces after commas. This is technically valid legacy CSS syntax, but deviates from what browsers display in DevTools and what designers expect to paste. The RGB output from chroma (line 72) also omits spaces. Inconsistency with the OKLCH format (which uses spaces) makes the copy output feel rough.\n\nFix: `hsl(${hue}, ${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%, 50%)` and similarly for RGB: `rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})`.\n\n---\n\n### NIT\n\n**N1 — `formatAllFormats` called without `useMemo` in TargetFormats**\n`app/src/components/TargetFormats/TargetFormats.tsx:23`\n\n`formatAllFormats(hue, saturation)` calls chroma 4× per render. Any parent re-render triggers redundant color conversions. Touch events (`onTouchStart`, `onTouchMove`, `onTouchEnd`) are absent. Hex, RGB, OKLCH conversions are correct. Out-of-gamut clamping is handled by chroma internally.\n- **State / Zustand**: No stale closures in store actions. `setTarget` atomically writes all four fields. <teammate-message teammate_id="feature-reviewer" color="blue" summary="feature-review done — 9 findings (0C/2H/3M/2L/2Nit)">
feature-review done — 9 findings (0C / 2H / 3M / 2L / 2Nit). Top issues: canvas crosshair never redraws when hue/sat change externally; canvas has no keyboard support or ARIA; FormatSelector uses raw `text-white` instead of design token.

---

## Findings

### HIGH

**H1 — Canvas crosshair is stale after external hue/sat changes**
`app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:193-198`

`handleCanvasRef` is a React callback ref assigned via `ref={handleCanvasRef}`. React fires callback refs only on mount/unmount — it does NOT re-invoke them when the callback function identity changes. `drawPicker` (which depends on `[hue, saturation]`) will update its closure, but `handleCanvasRef` is never re-called, so the canvas crosshair position is frozen at the values present when the popover first opened. If the user changes hue/saturation via external sliders while the popover is open, the canvas reads as correct but the crosshair is lying.

Fix: replace the ref callback pattern with a plain `ref={canvasRef}` and add a `useEffect(() => { if (canvasRef.current) drawPicker(canvasRef.current); }, [drawPicker])` to redraw on every hue/sat change.

---

**H2 — Canvas picker has no keyboard support and is inaccessible**
`app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:301-309`

The `<canvas>` element has no `tabIndex`, no `role`, no `aria-label`, and no keyboard event handlers. A keyboard-only user has no way to move the crosshair. The popover Trigger has an aria-label, but once focus enters the popover the canvas is a dead end. The text input below it is reachable, but the two-dimensional picker itself is fully inaccessible.

Fix: add `tabIndex={0}`, `role="application"`, `aria-label="Hue and saturation picker"`, and `onKeyDown` handlers that increment/decrement hue with arrow keys and saturation with shift+arrow.

---

### MEDIUM

**M1 — Design token violation: `text-white` in FormatSelector**
`app/src/components/FormatSelector/FormatSelector.tsx:31`

```ts
const btnActive = "bg-surface-active text-white";
```

`text-white` is a hardcoded color, not a design token. Per project rules and saved user feedback, all text should use semantic tokens (`text-text-primary`, `text-text-secondary`, etc.) rather than raw white. `text-text-primary` is 98% lightness and the correct substitute here.

Fix: change `text-white` → `text-text-primary`.

---

**M2 — Hardcoded `rgba()` values in boxShadow inline styles**
`app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:269-270`

```tsx
? "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.1), inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)"
: "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.15)",
```

Two hardcoded `rgba()` shadow values on the swatch circle. Project rules prohibit `rgba(` in component styles; design tokens must be used. The `border-stroke-grid` / `border-border-default` tokens exist for this.

Fix: move these to Tailwind `ring-*` / `shadow-*` utilities using CSS variable tokens, or at minimum use `var(--color-border-default)` style references.

---

**M3 — No ARIA live region for copy feedback in TargetFormats**
`app/src/components/TargetFormats/TargetFormats.tsx:33-51`

When a format button is clicked, the label text changes from e.g. "rgb(180,90,127)" to "Copied". This is purely visual — screen readers get no announcement. Users relying on assistive technology have no confirmation their action succeeded.

Fix: add `aria-live="polite"` and `aria-atomic="true"` to the value `<span>` (line 47), or wrap the entire format row container with a live region.

---

### LOW

**L1 — `oklch()` parser does not handle modern slash-alpha syntax**
`app/src/lib/colors.ts:24-27`

The regex `/oklch\(\s*([\d.]+%?)\s+([\d.]+)\s+([\d.]+)\s*\)/i` rejects `oklch(0.7 0.15 200 / 0.5)` (CSS Color Level 4 alpha syntax) — it returns `null` and the input is flagged invalid. A user pasting from a modern design tool that emits oklch with alpha would see a false parse failure.

Fix: extend the regex to optionally match `\s*(?:\/\s*[\d.]+%?)?\s*` before the closing paren, then ignore the alpha channel for hue/sat extraction (since the tool operates at fixed L=50).

---

**L2 — `formatTargetColor` HSL output omits spaces after separators**
`app/src/lib/colors.ts:69`

```ts
return `hsl(${hue},${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%,50%)`;
```

Produces `hsl(180,50%,50%)` — no spaces after commas. This is technically valid legacy CSS syntax, but deviates from what browsers display in DevTools and what designers expect to paste. The RGB output from chroma (line 72) also omits spaces. Inconsistency with the OKLCH format (which uses spaces) makes the copy output feel rough.

Fix: `hsl(${hue}, ${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%, 50%)` and similarly for RGB: `rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})`.

---

### NIT

**N1 — `formatAllFormats` called without `useMemo` in TargetFormats**
`app/src/components/TargetFormats/TargetFormats.tsx:23`

`formatAllFormats(hue, saturation)` calls chroma 4× per render. Any parent re-render triggers redundant color conversions. Low cost individually, but this component is visible during every palette interaction.

Fix: `const formats = useMemo(() => formatAllFormats(hue, saturation), [hue, saturation])`.

---

**N2 — Canvas has no touch support**
`app/src/components/TargetSwatch/TargetSwatch.tsx:301-309`

The picker only wires `onMouseDown` + document `mousemove`/`mouseup`. Touch events (`onTouchStart`, `onTouchMove`, `onTouchEnd`) are absent. The popover is usable on mobile (the text input still works), but the two-dimensional picker is mouse-only.

Not blocking for a desktop-first tool, but worth noting if mobile support is on the roadmap.

---

### Clean categories
- **Correctness (core parse/format)**: chroma.js `null` hue/sat from achromatic inputs is safely coerced to 0 via the `Math.round(isNaN(x) ? 0 : x)` chain — verified. Hex, RGB, OKLCH conversions are correct. Out-of-gamut clamping is handled by chroma internally.
- **State / Zustand**: No stale closures in store actions. `setTarget` atomically writes all four fields. No duplicated local state — `value` / `isInvalid` in `ColorInput` are legitimately local UI state, not duplicates of Zustand.
- **FormatSelector assumptions**: The file is purely new (not a rename); no prior assumptions were broken.
</teammate-message>

---

1	import chroma from "chroma-js";
2	
3	const WCAG_AA_CONTRAST = 4.5;
4	
5	export function getContrastColor(bgColor: string): string {
6	  const whiteContrast = chroma.contrast("white", bgColor);
7	  return whiteContrast < WCAG_AA_CONTRAST ? "#1d1d1d" : "#ffffff";
8	}
9	
10	export type ColorFormat = "hex" | "hsl" | "rgb" | "oklch" | "named";
11	
12	/**
13	 * Parse a color string and extract hue (0-360), saturation (0-1), and input format.
14	 * Supports hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch(), and named CSS colors.
15	 */
16	export function parseTargetColor(
17	  input: string,
18	): { hue: number; saturation: number; format: ColorFormat } | null {
19	  const trimmed = input.trim();
20	  if (!trimmed) return null;
21	
22	  try {
23	    // oklch() - chroma.js can't parse this natively
24	    const oklchMatch = trimmed.match(
25	      /oklch\(\s*([\d.]+%?)\s+([\d.]+)\s+([\d.]+)\s*\)/i,
26	    );
27	    if (oklchMatch) {
28	      let l = parseFloat(oklchMatch[1]);
29	      if (oklchMatch[1].endsWith("%")) l /= 100;
30	      const c = parseFloat(oklchMatch[2]);
31	      const h = parseFloat(oklchMatch[3]);
32	      const [hue, sat] = chroma.oklch(l, c, h).hsl();
33	      return {
34	        hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
35	        saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
36	        format: "oklch",
37	      };
38	    }
39	
40	    // Detect format before parsing
41	    const lower = trimmed.toLowerCase();
42	    let format: ColorFormat = "named";
43	    if (lower.startsWith("#")) format = "hex";
44	    else if (lower.startsWith("hsl")) format = "hsl";
45	    else if (lower.startsWith("rgb")) format = "rgb";
46	
47	    const [hue, sat] = chroma(trimmed).hsl();
48	    return {
49	      hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
50	      saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
51	      format,
52	    };
53	  } catch {
54	    return null;
55	  }
56	}
57	
58	/**
59	 * Format a color at hue/sat/L=50 in the given format.
60	 */
61	export function formatTargetColor(
62	  hue: number,
63	  saturation: number,
64	  format: ColorFormat,
65	): string {
66	  const color = chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5);
67	  switch (format) {
68	    case "hsl":
69	      return `hsl(${hue},${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%,50%)`;
70	    case "rgb": {
71	      const [r, g, b] = color.rgb();
72	      return `rgb(${r},${g},${b})`;
73	    }
74	    case "oklch":
75	      return toOklchString(color.hex());
76	    default:
77	      return color.hex();
78	  }
79	}
80	
81	export function toOklchString(hex: string): string {
82	  const [l, c, h] = chroma(hex).oklch();
83	  const lR = Math.round(l * 1000) / 1000;
84	  const cR = Math.round((c || 0) * 1000) / 1000;
85	  const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
86	  return `oklch(${lR} ${cR} ${hR})`;
87	}
88	
89	export interface AllFormats {
90	  hex: string;
91	  rgb: string;
92	  hsl: string;
93	  oklch: string;
94	}
95	
96	/**
97	 * Return all four string representations of a color at hue/sat/L=50.
98	 */
99	export function formatAllFormats(hue: number, saturation: number): AllFormats {
100	  return {
101	    hex: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "hex"),
102	    rgb: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "rgb"),
103	    hsl: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "hsl"),
104	    oklch: formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, "oklch"),
105	  };
106	}
107
```

306	            style={{ width: PICKER_SIZE, height: PICKER_SIZE }}
307	            onMouseDown={handleMouseDown}
308	          />
309	          <div className="text-text-faint mt-2 flex justify-between text-sm">
310	            <span>Hue {hue}°</span>
311	            <span>Sat {Math.round(saturation * 100)}%</span>
312	          </div>
313	          <ColorInput
314	            currentHex={currentHex}
315	            targetInput={targetInput}
316	            targetFormat={targetFormat}
317	            hue={hue}
318	            saturation={saturation}
319	            onApply={setTarget}
320	          />
321	          <Popover.Arrow className="fill-surface-overlay" />
322	        </Popover.Content>
323	      </Popover.Portal>
324	    </Popover.Root>
325	  );
326	}
327
```
</details></tool-use>

Before creating, check if a similar item already exists. If a close match is found, ask the user if they still want to create a new one.

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes → likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation — do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

## Arguments

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

## Determine Next ID

Run the ID assignment script:

```bash
bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/next-id.sh <TYPE> ./board
```

This scans all directories (including `board/archive/` and `.doc-ids`) and returns the next available 4-digit zero-padded ID.

## Create the File

Use the correct directory, frontmatter, and naming convention:

| Type | Directory | Filename Example |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| task | `board/tasks/` | `TASK-0014_add-batch-processing.md` |
| epic | `board/planning/` | `EPIC-0009_batch-mode.md` |
| adr | `board/planning/` | `ADR-0002_chose-webgpu-over-wasm.md` |
| note | `board/planning/` | `NOTE-0001_explore-wasm-option.md` |
| reference | `board/reference/` | `REFE-0007_webgpu-overview.md` |
| resource | `board/resources/` | `RESO-0001_webgpu-article.md` |

## Frontmatter Templates

Task:
```yaml
---
type: task
status: backlog
priority: 2
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Epic:
```yaml
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

ADR:
```yaml
---
type: adr
status: active
context_date: YYYY-MM-DD
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Note:
```yaml
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Reference:
```yaml
---
type: reference
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Resource (ask user for URL):
```yaml
---
type: resource
status: inbox
url: <url>
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

## Naming Rules

- Convert title to kebab-case for filename
- Task titles MUST be imperative mood (start with a verb). If the user gives a noun phrase, rewrite it (e.g., "batch processing" -> "Add batch processing support")
- Use today's date for `created`

Show the file path and ask if the user wants to add more detail to the body.
